history.summary: Dedicated Bottles setup with current Widescreen Fix, display-independent resolution, GE-Proton 11-1 NT sync configuration, native DirectX 9 repair, verification, rollback and Steam shortcut support.
Adds checksum-verified Steam library artwork and documents the confirmed Steam Overlay limitation for Flatpak non-Steam games; ineffective preload forwarding is not retained.
Defaults new NFSMW bottles to win64 and installs d3dx9 plus d3dcompiler_47 freshly through Bottles Winetricks; existing bottles are never converted automatically.
Promotes the player-confirmed working win64 GE-Proton bottle to the canonical NFSMW default, rejects an existing bottle with the wrong architecture, and verifies win64 explicitly.
Makes the public installation path explicitly create win64 from the start; no win32 precursor, conversion, or migration is required or supported.

Adds optional player-confirmed direct Steam integration with one canonical shortcut, Overlay, FPS counter, complete artwork and display-independent pre-launch preparation; shared-prefix risk is documented.
Initializes required Proton bookkeeping without copying dependencies, preventing first-launch tracked-files failures while continuing to share the canonical Bottles prefix.

Adds the player-confirmed Xbox 360 Stuff 4.1 and HD Reflections installer with payload verification, preserves automatic display selection, and documents confirmed Xbox controller support without replacing the player's mapping.
Prevents stale Steam Running state by replacing the pre-launch Bottles registry command with a bounded offline registry edit that cannot leave Wine services behind.
